title: Dryden And Pope.
content:
Genius of Dryden and of Pope,
            Both did take a mighty scope,
            The first he virgil did translate,
            The second showed us Troys fate.
            On English themes they oft did sing
            And high their muses flight did wing.
meaning:
This evocative piece by James McIntyre, titled "Dryden And Pope.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
